notes: Initial canonical vocabulary seeded from HY-IG v2 reference pair (per META-RPD) after Wave-5 reproducibility-audit Axis 1 exposed arbitrary caption-prefix variation across pages and pairs. Four prefixes cover the full taxonomy: data description, investor impact, interpretation guide, honest caveat. Every caption on every portal page MUST begin with one of these four; caption body wording remains the author's discretion. Ace owns; Ray reviews tone at handoff per META-ELI5.

What this shows:.when_to_use: 1-line description of what the chart/table/metric is displaying — the literal data content, not its implication. Use when the reader needs to know WHAT they are looking at before interpretation.
What this shows:.example: What this shows: HMM stress-probability (0 to 1) computed on HY-IG OAS spread, overlaid with NBER recession shading for 2006-2025.

Why this matters:.when_to_use: Investor-impact takeaway — the 'so what' for a non-quant stakeholder. Use for APP-SE5 Universal Takeaway Captions in the Strategy Confidence section, Story-page historical-episode bullets (RES-9), and any KPI card delta interpretation.
Why this matters:.example: Why this matters: the strategy excels in credit-driven crises (GFC, COVID) but offers limited protection during pure rate-shock selloffs (2022).

How to read it:.when_to_use: Guide to reading a complex chart element — axis convention, color encoding, threshold lines, shading semantics. Use for Evidence 8-element 'observation' captions and RES-2 translation bridges on first use of a quant construct.
How to read it:.example: How to read it: the dashed line at 0.5 is the decision threshold; probability above that triggers the REDUCE signal; shaded bands are NBER recessions.

Caveat:.when_to_use: Honest caution about sample size, OOS window, regime coverage, or model limitation. Use wherever a result could be over-interpreted by a non-quant reader (per RES-EP1 element 7).
Caveat:.example: Caveat: OOS window is 8 years (2017-2025) and covers one credit crisis; post-2026 behaviour under a rate-shock-only regime is extrapolated, not tested.
